meta.title: Multiprogrammed batch processing
meta.description: Batch processing simulator with multiprogramming and interrupts.

nav.tagline: Batch simulator with multiprogramming, queues, and keyboard shortcuts (E, W, P, C).
nav.clockHint: Increases one step per tick while the simulation runs.
nav.sectionControlsHint: Pause, resume, or reset the run.
nav.runMenuHint: Set how many jobs to generate (batches of up to 4), then start.

setup.title: Initial setup
setup.description: Enter how many jobs to generate. Jobs are grouped into batches of up to 4. Each job’s ID, estimated time, and operation are generated automatically.
setup.hint: Try 4–40 jobs to see multiple batches.

dashboard.tickHint: Lower tick values run faster. You can also type the interval in milliseconds.
dashboard.clockHint: Increases by one on each tick while the simulation is running.
dashboard.tooltipPause: Pause the timer and CPU ticks (P).
dashboard.completedBody: You can return to setup to run another simulation.

columns.queueEmpty: Queue empty: CPU is on the first job of the batch or the queue is empty.
columns.queueCpuOnly: No queued jobs: the only ready job of this batch is on the CPU column.

empty.beforeStart: Top bar: play menu → job count → Start. Batches and the ready queue appear here.
empty.cpuIdle: When a process holds the CPU, its full card and progress bar appear here.
empty.noFinished: Completed jobs (success or error) land here with their results.

keys.title: Shortcuts
keys.e: I/O interrupt: running process moves to the end of the batch queue.
keys.w: Error: terminates the current process with ERROR in results.
keys.focusHint: Shortcuts work when the page is focused.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The requested path does not exist in this application.
